text: Small object detection in remote sensing imagery remains a significant challenge due to dense background noise, limited pixel information, and the diminutive scale of targets. While modern deep learning frameworks have advanced general object detection, their performance on these specialized datasets often falls short. In this paper, we propose LAR-YOLOv8, an enhanced detection framework designed specifically to address these limitations. Our methodology introduces three primary innovations: first, we integrate a dual-branch attention mechanism and Vision Transformer blocks into the backbone to refine local feature extraction and maximize global feature representation. Second, we develop an attention-guided bi-directional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) that utilizes a dynamic sparse attention mechanism to capture high-resolution details from shallow layers while employing top-down paths to supervise multi-scale feature fusion. Finally, we introduce the RIOU loss function, which improves bounding box regression by ensuring better shape consistency and preventing loss degradation in complex scenarios. Extensive evaluations on the NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 achieves superior mean Average Precision (mAP), particularly for small objects, while maintaining a competitive balance between parameter count and real-time processing speeds. These results validate the efficacy of the proposed architectural enhancements for robust remote sensing analysis.
